scenario: Clean style-only prompt, no source echo
sourceBodies: Hi Marcus, thanks for the intro. Happy to chat Tuesday about the rollout. Best, Sam
derived:
- Keep replies to 3-6 short lines.
- Open with a quick greeting, close with a brief sign-off.
- Be direct; lead with the answer.
- Avoid corporate buzzwords.

scenario: Greeting and sign-off habits, lowercase, no names
sourceBodies: Hey Priya, great call earlier. I'll send the deck over by Friday. Cheers, Sam
derived:
- Greet casually and warmly.
- Prefer one short paragraph over long blocks.
- Use plain wording; skip jargon.
- If a message can be one sentence, make it one sentence.

scenario: Small style figures are allowed (not flagged as numbers)
sourceBodies: Quick one: can we move the sync to 2pm? Thanks
derived:
- Aim for 2-4 sentences.
- Lead with the ask.
- Stay friendly but concise.

sourceBodies: Following up on the proposal. Let me know what you think. Best
derived:
- Use bullet points when listing more than two items.
- Avoid exclamation marks.
- Close with a low-key sign-off.

sourceBodies: Hi there, reach me anytime. Best, Sam
derived:
- Be concise.
- Sign off and share contact at sam@acme.com.

sourceBodies: Here is the link to book time. Thanks
derived:
- Keep it short.
- Offer the booking link https://cal.com/sam when proposing a call.

sourceBodies: Following up from our chat. Best
derived:
- Be direct.
- Mention acme.com when relevant.

sourceBodies: Sending over the quote now. Thanks
derived:
- Stay concise.
- Reference the $45,000 figure up front.

- Be brief.
- Offer to call at 415-555-1234.

scenario: Echoes a proper noun from the source mail (Stripe)
sourceBodies: Thanks for the Stripe integration docs, super helpful. Best, Sam
derived: - Write the way you did about Stripe — technical and concise.

scenario: Quotes a 6+ word verbatim phrase from the source
sourceBodies: I wanted to circle back on the pricing proposal we discussed last week.
derived: - circle back on the pricing proposal we discussed.

sourceBodies: See you at the 2026 kickoff. Thanks
derived:
- Keep it crisp.
- Mention the 2026 plan when relevant.

scenario: Echoes a company name from the source (Acme)
sourceBodies: Following up with the Acme team on next steps. Best
derived: - Match the tone you used with the Acme account.

scenario: Clean prompt even though source is full of entities
sourceBodies: Hi Dr. Chen, the Meridian Labs contract at $32k is ready. Call me at 212-555-0100. — Sam, sam@acme.com
derived:
- Be warm but brief.
- Lead with the key point.
- Avoid hype and template phrasing.
- Close with a simple sign-off.
